merge_reason: No mergeable output exists because the first live attempt was refused before contributor execution, validator acceptance, replay, and aggregation could produce one accepted merged adapter state.
publish_reason: Publication is refused because the lane did not earn a mergeable local snapshot. The retained workflow plan still points at `local_publish/openagents_swarm_local_open_adapter/first-swarm-local-snapshot` via `psionic-mlx-workflows::MlxWorkflowWorkspace::publish_bundle`, but that publish path stays planned-only until accepted contributor outputs, replay receipts, aggregation, and promotion truth all exist.

closeout_gates.detail: The evidence bundle records a refused first live attempt before remote execution began, so the exact two-node lane never produced contributor execution receipts for both required roles.
The contributor rows remain `validator_posture = not_executed`, so no accepted validator disposition exists for merge or publication.
Replay receipts remain absent because contributor execution never started and the lane requires replay receipts per accepted contribution.
Aggregation never ran under `aggregation.open_adapter.mean_delta` because no accepted contribution set existed to aggregate.
The retained evidence bundle ends with `promotion_outcome.disposition = no_promotion`, so no local snapshot was earned for publication.

proved: The first swarm lane now closes out with one deterministic machine-legible no-merge and no-publish report instead of leaving the end state implicit.
The closeout stays bound to the exact swarm contract, trusted-LAN topology contract, rehearsal report, retained evidence bundle, and workflow publish expectation for the Mac-plus-RTX-4080 lane.

The report names the exact local snapshot surface that would be used once the lane earns mergeable outputs: `psionic-mlx-workflows::MlxWorkflowWorkspace::publish_bundle` targeting one local Hugging Face-style snapshot directory.
surprises: The publish surface itself is not the blocker for the first swarm lane. The blocker is still the missing live contributor, validator, replay, and aggregation receipt set.
The lane already has enough retained planning truth to name the exact publish directory and repo identifier without widening the claim into a fake published snapshot.
remaining_blockers: Earn one real two-node contributor execution receipt set for the exact trusted-LAN lane.
Retain validator acceptance, replay, and aggregation receipts for both contributors under the frozen swarm governance contract.
Earn `promotion_outcome = promoted` before attempting local snapshot publication.
next_steps: Lift the current `no_go` gate only after the exact two-node lane can emit live contributor execution receipts instead of a refused bundle.
Wire accepted contributor outputs through the existing merge and aggregation path, then re-run closeout to determine whether a local snapshot is actually earned.
Only after merge and promotion are truthful should the Mac publisher role call `psionic-mlx-workflows::MlxWorkflowWorkspace::publish_bundle` for `first-swarm-local-snapshot`.
claim_boundary: This closeout report records the end state of the current first swarm trusted-LAN attempt. It proves explicit no-merge truth, explicit publish-refused truth, and the exact local publish surface that would be used after a later accepted mergeable outcome. It does not claim a successful two-node live execution, accepted contributor receipts, aggregation completion, or a published local snapshot.
